skill.name: architect-review
skill.description: Master software architect specializing in modern architecture patterns, clean architecture, microservices, event-driven systems, and DDD. Reviews system designs and code changes for architectural integrity, scalability, and maintainability.
skill.summary: Expert AI architect for reviewing system designs, evaluating architecture patterns, and ensuring scalable maintainable software systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 19 potential cryptographic algorithm patterns and 2 system reconnaissance patterns. All findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the skill file (SKILL.md) is pure documentation with no executable code. The pattern matches occur in text describing security concepts (encryption, JWT, OAuth2) and observability practices. No actual security risks, network access, file system operations, or external command execution present.

content.user_title: Review system architecture and design patterns
content.value_statement: Building scalable software systems requires expert architectural guidance. This skill provides comprehensive architecture reviews, pattern evaluation, and design recommendations to ensure your system remains maintainable and future-proof.
actual_capabilities: Evaluate architecture decisions against modern patterns like microservices, event-driven design, and clean architecture
Assess system designs for scalability, resilience, and maintainability characteristics
Identify architectural anti-patterns and recommend specific refactoring approaches
Review microservice boundaries and bounded context definitions using DDD principles
Analyze distributed system designs for proper fault tolerance and observability
Document architecture decisions using ADR format and C4 model diagrams
limitations: Requires sufficient system context and requirements to provide accurate architectural assessment
Cannot validate architecture without access to actual implementation details or code
Recommendations are guidance only and require team validation for specific constraints
Does not replace human architect judgment for critical business or compliance decisions

use_cases.title: Microservices Architecture Review
use_cases.description: Review microservice design for proper service boundaries, data isolation, and communication patterns. Ensures services follow single responsibility principle and maintain loose coupling.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers designing or refactoring microservice systems

use_cases.title: System Design Evaluation
use_cases.description: Evaluate new feature or system designs for scalability, performance, and maintainability. Identifies potential bottlenecks and single points of failure before implementation.
use_cases.target_user: Tech leads planning major system changes or new product features

use_cases.title: Architecture Compliance Check
use_cases.description: Assess code changes and Pull Requests for architectural consistency. Ensures implementations follow established patterns and do not introduce technical debt.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering managers maintaining architecture standards across teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this system design for architectural soundness. Current setup: [describe your system architecture]. Key concerns: [list specific concerns like scalability, maintainability, or specific patterns]. Provide feedback on potential issues and recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting initial feedback on a system design or architecture document

prompt_templates.title: Microservice Boundary Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these service boundaries for proper bounded contexts. Services: [list services and their responsibilities]. Data owned by each: [describe data ownership]. Communication patterns: [describe how services communicate]. Identify boundary violations and suggest improvements using DDD principles.
prompt_templates.scenario: Defining or refactoring microservice boundaries in a distributed system

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Pattern Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare architecture patterns for my use case. Requirements: [list functional requirements]. Constraints: [list constraints like budget, team size, timeline]. Current scale: [describe current and expected scale]. Evaluate event-driven vs request-response vs hybrid approaches with tradeoffs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing between architecture patterns for a new system or major refactor

prompt_templates.title: Technical Debt Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess architectural technical debt in this system. Codebase context: [describe system age, known issues, pain points]. Recent problems: [list incidents or bugs related to architecture]. Prioritize debt items by impact and effort. Create remediation plan with quick wins and long-term improvements.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning architectural improvements for an existing system with accumulated technical debt

output_examples.input: Review this microservice design: User Service handles authentication and user profiles. Order Service manages orders and calls User Service for user data. Both services share a single PostgreSQL database.
output: ARCHITECTURAL VIOLATION: Shared database violates microservice data isolation principle
RISK: Tight coupling prevents independent scaling and deployment
RECOMMENDATION: Implement database-per-service pattern with User Service owning user data
RECOMMENDATION: Use API calls or events for Order Service to access user information
RECOMMENDATION: Consider eventual consistency if strong consistency is not required

output_examples.input: Should we use event sourcing for our e-commerce order system? We have 10K orders per day and need audit trail.
output: EVENT SOURCING FIT: Good candidate due to audit trail requirement
BENEFIT: Complete order history enables order status reconstruction and debugging
BENEFIT: Supports CQRS for optimized read models and reporting
TRADEOFF: Increased complexity in event schema evolution and migration
TRADEOFF: Requires event store infrastructure and snapshot strategy
ALTERNATIVE: Consider simple audit logging if full event reconstruction is not needed

best_practices: Document all significant architecture decisions using Architecture Decision Records (ADR) with context, options considered, and consequences
Design for failure by implementing circuit breakers, bulkheads, and proper timeout patterns in distributed systems
Establish clear bounded contexts and service boundaries early to prevent tightly coupled monolithic distributed systems
anti_patterns: Distributed monolith - microservices that share databases or have synchronous chatty communication creating tight coupling
Premature optimization - implementing complex patterns like event sourcing or CQRS without validating actual scale requirements
Architecture astronaut - over-engineering with unnecessary abstraction layers that reduce code clarity and increase maintenance burden

faq.question: When should I request an architecture review?
faq.answer: Request reviews for major system changes, new feature designs with cross-system impact, microservice boundary changes, technology stack decisions, or when addressing scalability or performance issues. Minor bug fixes or localized changes typically do not need architectural review.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide for a useful review?
faq.answer: Provide system context including current architecture diagram or description, business requirements and constraints, expected scale and growth, existing pain points or incidents, and specific areas of concern. More context enables more accurate and actionable recommendations.

faq.question: Can this skill review my actual code for architectural compliance?
faq.answer: Yes, provide code snippets, Pull Request descriptions, or module structure. The skill evaluates code organization, dependency patterns, separation of concerns, and adherence to architectural principles. Include context about intended architecture for accurate assessment.

faq.question: How do I implement recommended architectural changes?
faq.answer: Prioritize changes by impact and risk. Start with low-risk improvements that provide quick wins. Create incremental migration plans for larger changes. Use feature flags and gradual rollout strategies. Document each step and validate with testing before proceeding to next phase.

faq.question: What architecture patterns does this skill support?
faq.answer: Supports modern patterns including microservices, event-driven architecture, clean architecture, hexagonal architecture, domain-driven design, CQRS, event sourcing, serverless, and cloud-native patterns. Also covers SOLID principles and common design patterns.

faq.question: Can this help with cloud provider selection and multi-cloud strategy?
faq.answer: Yes, provides guidance on cloud provider patterns for AWS, Azure, and GCP. Discusses multi-cloud and hybrid cloud considerations including portability tradeoffs, vendor lock-in risks, and operational complexity. Helps evaluate serverless vs container vs VM deployment options.
